source_title: InSpace Balloon Implant Surgical Technique Guide
text: as the implant is inflated Note: You will feel some resistance as you depress the syringe to fill the balloon, especially if a long length or a small diameter extension tubing is used Safety lock button Once the recommended final volume is reached, keep the delivery system stable, slide the red safety lock button forward. Perform gentle maneuvers of the patient’s arm to verify correct positioning and stability of the implant, prior to sealing and detachment. Once correct positioning is confirmed, release the red backstopper and pull back the protective sheath which will expose the implant. Note: Once the red backstopper is released and you have retracted the protective sheath you are committed to the positioning of the implant (any movement would risk possible breakage or puncture of the implant) Release lever Rotate the green release lever clockwise. This will seal the implant and leave it in situ. Once the implant is sealed, withdraw the delivery system and verify again that the implant is stable, in its proper location and does not interfere with passive maneuvers of the shoulder’s range of motion. Patient preparation Patients should receive prophylactic broad-spectrum antibiotics per local/institutional guidelines for orthopedic implants prior to InSpace implantation. Safety lock button During insertion, release and filling of the balloon, the red safety lock button should be facing the underside of the acromion. Release lever Positioning and set-up A standard arrangement for arthroscopic rotator cuff repair arthroscopy is used. Either beach chair or the lateral decubitus position is appropriate. Attention: InSpace package does not contain the items below. These are required for the procedure and should be readily available prior to commencing the procedure: • Sterile, disposable Luer-Lock syringe, 50 – 60cc • Sterile standard measuring probe • Sterile, disposable extension tube (optional but recommended) • Sterile normal saline solution (sodium chloride 0.9%) for inflation of the InSpace implant• Sterile, 3-way valve (optional; i.e., 3-way stopcock) Sports Medicine This document is intended solely for the use of healthcare professionals. A surgeon must always rely on his or her own professional clinical judgment when deciding whether to use a particular product when treating a particular patient. We do not dispense medical advice and recommend that surgeons be trained in the use of any particular product before using it in surgery. The information presented is intended to demonstrate Stryker’s products. A surgeon must always refer to the package insert, product label and/or instructions for use, including the instructions for cleaning and sterilization (if applicable), before using any of Stryker’s products.
